title: Rumble imperceptible on macOS / modern Bluetooth controllers: N64 motor strobing needs pulse-stretching
author: fyreatwill
body:
On macOS with a DualShock 4 over Bluetooth, rumble appears completely dead in-game, and the Input Editor's rumble Test gives only a fraction of a motor turn. Diagnosed two compounding causes:

1. **libultraship sends zero-duration rumble**, which doesn't persist on sdl2-compat/SDL3 stacks (Homebrew macOS). Filed upstream with a suggested fix: Kenix3/libultraship#1140.

2. **SF64 strobes the Rumble Pak motor.** The game modulates rumble strength with PWM: `Player_Update` sets the rumble flag only on even frames (`gGameFrameCount % 2`, fox_play.c), and `Controller_Rumble` (sys_joybus.c) sends `osMotorStop` on every frame the flag isn't set. A real Rumble Pak's motor averages out 16 ms on/off pulses; a modern controller receiving start/stop commands every frame (over Bluetooth, with command latency and ~100 ms motor spin-up) never gets moving. Short events are worst: charged-shot rumble is a 5-frame timer (~83 ms), physically imperceptible on a DualShock even with perfect delivery.

**Fix that worked locally** (macOS 26, sdl2-compat 2.32.70/SDL3 3.4.12, DS4 v1 over BT): pulse-stretching in the `__osMotorAccess` shim —
- re-send `StartRumble()` on every start call (keeps the effect armed);
- swallow stops arriving < 80 ms after the last start (merges PWM strobing into continuous rumble);
- enforce a ~350 ms minimum pulse so 5-frame blips register.

With both changes, Test, death, cutscene, and short-blip rumble all work on the setup above.

Possibly related: #239 (no rumble on Switch — same strobing would affect any backend where stops apply immediately) and #198 (rumble stuck on — pulse-stretching also centralizes stop handling).

Happy to PR the shim if there's interest.

title: No MacOS build
author: PGtheVRguy
body: There isnt any prebuilt MacOS release under the releases tab and all references to a MacOS build on the readme just reach a 404

title: [Feature Request] Online VS mode
author: Marshgames14

title: Add low level controller mapping?
author: UrbisR
body:
I am not sure if I am interpreting the issue correctly. I am trying to play on Linux with an 8bitdo N64 mod kit controller, connected while in 'Switch' mode over bluetooth (a similar thing happens in the other 'D' mode)
It appears that the controller is automatically mapped using SDL to generic button names as if it was, say, a modern Xbox controller. Then when I use the built-in button mapper in Starship (the UI is way too small by the way), it works, but the button names as I press them make no sense! For example, C-up is called the 'view' button. Importantly, C-right is not detected at all and I cannot map it. In 'D' mode it is a different set of buttons that are not detected.

So, I figure SDL is trying to map the N64 controller to an Xbox layout as best as it knows how to, but doesn't have a place to put C-right and just loses that button.
I then tried 'SDL2 Gamepad Tool' and am able to map all the N64 buttons to something on the generic Xbox layout (I assigned C-up and C-right to the joystick click-in buttons for example). I believe I saved this layout to the system, rebooted the PC, but it still does not work in Starship. I gather that Starship takes it's config from 'gamecontrollerdb.txt' instead of the OS? I am not sure how I could edit this file if it is even possible. This whole issue I think could be avoided if I could just assign the generic 'button 1', 'button 2' etc. inputs right inside Starship.

Or maybe I am misunderstanding the issue, I am not sure.

title: Unable to select VS mode
author: khalleu
body: Hey! Really simple question, is VS mode playable? I've played through the game once, but I'm still unable to select it for some reason.

title: Does Anyone Know What Happened to SonicDCER?
author: Arquivista
body:
Has anyone heard what happened to SonicDCER's GitHub account? Over the past few days, both his profile and all of his repositories have completely disappeared. Every recompilation project he was working on seems to be gone as well.

I'm curious if anyone knows whether the projects were moved elsewhere, taken down, or if SonicDCER has commented on the situation.

title: 2 Feature Requests: Mouse aiming and Vulkan Support
author: therealkiddude

title: Crash on Macbeth near checkpoint (MacOS)
author: joaomarcosfagundessilva
body:
Hi,

Most of the times (not always) the game will crash on Macbeth near the checkpoint. I was trying to narrow down the source of the crash and I noticed that when I turned the bluetooth off (I play with a SN30 pro 2 controller) it went away, even when I reconnected the controller, either via cable or bluetooth. It might be a coincidence though. I also noticed the rumble only works if the controller is plugged in via cable.

The crash report is attached, I was playing on a Macbook pro 2021 M1 (Sequoia 15.6.1) with the SN30 pro 2 via bluetooth on an external monitor (an LG TV).

[crash_report.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28614947/crash_report.txt)

title: Can't bind mouse buttons to controls
author: ToasterGen
body: I want to bind A and B to Left Click and Right Click, but Starship just won't let me! please patch this, Star Fox 64 is my all-time favorite game and I'd hate to not be able to play it again.

title: Can't change backend graphics on starship.cfg.json
author: nzxweb
body: I'm having issues with DirectX 11 where the game keeps crashing. I tried changing the starship.cfg.json file, but when I launch the game, it reverts back to DirectX 11. Even with the file set to read-only, the game still only opens in DirectX 11.

title: [Feature Request] Moveable arwing effects
author: sabre230
body: It would be really helpful for modders to be able to move the position of the effects on the arwing like the laser spawn, engine glow, and boost trail locations.

title: [Feature Request] D-PAD arwing aerobatics
author: sabre230
body: Would be nice to have feature parity with the recomp to allow the D-PAD controls to make arwing aerobatics, or even assign them to their own buttons for the sake of accessibility.

title: Crash on Zoness Boss
author: U-Flame
body: Tried out the Playtest version for the Switch port, and was stuck on the boss for a bit, as he would submerge the moment the exhaust pipes were bombed so it was difficult to deal any damage. Unsure if that's intended behavior, been forever since I played. But I took a bit of a break and came back to see the ol' "game closed because an error occurred" screen, not sure how long it took from when I paused to when it crashed, but I included the atmosphere crash log, and the Starship log if it helps. I'll try again later to see if it crashes again, and if it does, I'll give the Barnard Alfa version a try to see if it happens on the stable build or not. But for now, hope this is helpful. So far, still using the same Playtest build, it has not yet crashed again. I also figured out what I was doing wrong with the fight so it turned out to be behaving normal as well. Thus far, seems the crash was a one-time occurrence. [01778763688_01aa7cb9f4128000.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27761844/01778763688_01aa7cb9f4128000.log) [Starship.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27761852/Starship.log)

title: All connected controllers reenabled on restart
author: Unixsystem13
body: Issue: Controllers unchecked on the controller config screen will be re-checked and reenabled when restarting Starship To recreate: 1) Connect 2+ controllers (or virtual controllers) 2) Disable one of the controllers on the controller config screen 3) Close Starship 4) On reopening Starship, all connected controllers are defaulted to enabled Why this is an issue: With some controllers not optimized for PC like the 8bitdo 64, an Xbox controller emulator like BetterJoy is needed to make the controller show up as an Xbox controller in order to get rumble recognized in-game. This however leaves both the emulated controller and the original controller active in Windows and therefore in the Starship config menu, but both use different button layouts. When both controllers are active, pressing one button on the physical controller will register as inputs on both of the software controllers. It is a minor annoyance to go back into settings every time Starship is rebooted and uncheck the original, non-emulated controller but it is an annoyance. The same issue is present on Ghostship and presumably all Harbormasters projects. To solve: Just make the Enabled Controllers checkboxes save their state the same as everything else does when closing the program.

title: Controller Binds menu not accepting inputs
author: JelloToad64
body: The control menu for some reason is not accepting inputs, so i cannot remap keys on my keyboard or controller. the default binds settings still work, and the game seems to receive inputs when playing, but not on the configuration menu for controls.

title: Area 6 Bugged--Level stuck in place, no enemies spawning, music cuts off
author: SupahDrivah1234
body: Game is stuck on Area 6, whether playing normally or via debug level skips. Arwing moves normally but level-scrolling is stuck with no enemies spawning and the music is cut off. Can't progress after this normally.

title: [Bug] MacOS: Crash when defeating boss in Sector Y
author: AJ292
body:
On MacOS, using the V1.1 ROM, the game immediately crashes as soon as one of the two Sector Y bosses is defeated. The mini cutscene that shows them blowing up begins to play and then the game crashes. It doesn't seem to matter which of the two bosses is defeated first. 

Running MacOS 15.7.4 on version 0.1.0 of the app.

title: Remove debug console for non-debug sessions
author: Shituation
body: The terminal window that opens is annoying for couch gaming. You should turn it off when compiling, so the users don't have a game window and a console window when opening the game.

title: Not launching the game in fullscreen
author: Shituation
